---
page-layout: full

title: "Jun Yamamoto"
subtitle: PhD candidate in Network Science <br> Department of Network and Data Science, Central European University
image: assets/images/prof_pic.jpeg
about:
  id: profile
  template: solana
  image-width: 8em
  links:
    - text: '{{< fa solid file title=File logo label="File logo" >}} CV'
      href: assets/cv_jyamamoto.pdf
      aria-label: "CV logo"
    - text: '{{< fa envelope title="Envelope logo" label="Envelope logo" >}} Mail'
      href: "mailto:jun.j.yamamoto@gmail.com"
      aria-label: "Envelope logo"
    - text: '{{< ai orcid title="ORCID logo" label="ORCID logo" >}} ORCID'
      href: "https://orcid.org/0009-0009-5089-3269"
      aria-label: "ORCID logo"
    - text: '{{< ai google-scholar title="Google Scholar logo" label="Google Scholar logo" >}} Google Scholar'
      href: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=ILnN9b4AAAAJ"
      aria-label: "Google Scholar logo"
    - text: '{{< fa brands bluesky >}}'
      href: "https://bsky.app/profile/jymmt.bsky.social"
      aria-label: "BlueSky logo"
    # - text: '{{< fa brands twitter >}}'
    #   href: "https://twitter.com/__jymmt__"
    #   aria-label: "Twitter logo"
    - text: '{{< fa brands linkedin >}}'
      href: "https://www.linkedin.com/in/jun-yamamoto-579916199"
      aria-label: "LinkedIn logo"
html:
  self-contained: true
---

::: {#profile}
:::

I am a PhD candidate in Network Science in the [Department of Network and Data Science](https://www.ceu.edu/departments/network-data-science) at [Central European University](https://www.ceu.edu/) in Vienna. 
I am interested in the interplay between network structure and dynamics, particularly in physical networks and their coarse-grained representations. My PhD project focuses on understanding how physical constraints influence network topology and dynamics, including Laplacian eigenmode localization and synchronization.

My research interests and ongoing projects include:

- [**physical networks**](research.qmd#physnets) --- topology-physicality correlations, Laplacian dynamics, spectral theory; 

- [**scaling theory for networks**](research.qmd#netscale) --- scale-invariance, hierarchical models, criticality, long-range degree-correlation;

- [**network synchronization**](research.qmd#netsync) --- modular and hierarchical synchronization, coarse-graining, and experimental realizations of oscillator networks.

I am supervised by Prof. [Márton Pósfai](https://people.ceu.edu/marton_posfai) and Prof. [János Kertész](https://people.ceu.edu/janos_kertesz).


※日本語のプロファイルは[こちら](https://researchmap.jp/jun_yamamoto)を参照して下さい．

::::{.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8 style="text-align: left;"}

## Education

- **PhD in Network Science**, In progress. <br> 
  *Department of Network and Data Science, Central European University*   
  Vienna, Austria.

- **MSc in Mathematics**, **2023**. <br>
  *School of Mathematical Sciences, Queen Mary University of London*   
  London, UK.

- **BEng in Applied Physics**, **2022**. <br>
  *Department of Applied Physics, Faculty of Engineering, Hokkaido University*   
  Sapporo, Japan.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 style="text-align: left;"}

## Interests

- Complex networks
- Network physicality
- Network dynamics
- Scale-invariance
- Critical phenomena

:::

::: {.g-col-12 style="text-align: left;"}

## News

- **2026-07-16**: I co-organized a networking event for Japanese researchers in Austria at the Official Residence of the Japanese Ambassador in Vienna, Austria. 

- **2026-06~2026-07**: I visited the [Barabasi Lab](https://barabasi.com/science) at Northeastern University in Boston, USA, for a research stay as an [AccelNet-MultiNet fellow](https://www.accelnet-multinet.org/).

- **2026-06-02**: [Physical Networks, IV edition](https://sites.google.com/view/physnet26), a NetSci satellite event for physical networks was held at NetSci26 in Boston. 

- **2026-02-17**: I presented a poster on localization transitions in Laplacian eigenmodes of physical networks at the [Network Science Seminar AY2025/26](https://www.network-science-seminar.com/activities/2025) in Kyoto, Japan.

:::

::::
